metadata.title: Build a Prompt Card Library with Claude

beats.label: Hook — prompts as disposable
beats.narration: Somewhere in your chat history is a prompt that worked. You spent twenty minutes refining it. You closed the tab. Three weeks later you need it again — and you start from scratch. That's the cost of treating prompts as disposable.

shots.notes: Conversation thread icon with 'The prompt that worked...' label, then a DELETE icon or fade-out. Below: clock icon '3 weeks later' -> blank prompt input. CRIMSON annotation: 'Starting from scratch. Again.' Teardown palette.

beats.label: Claude command
beats.narration: The seed command takes any successful prompt plus its output and extracts it into the 8-field prompt card format — task, context, constraints, format, examples, criteria, failure modes, version notes.

props.command: claude "Extract this prompt-output pair into an 8-field prompt card: Task, Context, Constraints, Output Format, Examples, Evaluation Criteria, Failure Modes, Version Notes. Be specific on every field." < prompt_and_output.md

beats.label: Python code
beats.narration: The script reads the prompt and output, extracts the 8-field card via Claude, writes it to a markdown library file, and updates an index with the card ID, purpose, and status.

props.code:
import subprocess, sys, pathlib
pair = open(sys.argv[1]).read()
extract_cmd = (
  "Extract into 8-field prompt card: Task, Context, "
  "Constraints, Output Format, Examples, Eval Criteria,"
  " Failure Modes, Version Notes. Specific on every field."
)
card = subprocess.run(
  ["claude", extract_cmd], input=pair,
  capture_output=True, text=True
).stdout
library = pathlib.Path("prompt_library.md")
library.write_text(library.read_text() + card)

beats.label: 8-field card
beats.narration: The 8-field card captures everything a future user needs to reuse the prompt — including the failure modes and version notes that are almost always missing from saved prompts.

shots.notes: Formatted prompt card. Card ID: WRITE-01. Eight labeled fields stacked: Task / Context / Constraints / Output Format / Examples / Evaluation Criteria / Failure Modes / Version Notes. GOLD highlight on 'Failure Modes' and 'Version Notes' — labeled 'usually missing'. Teardown palette, CREAM background.

beats.label: The change — merge two cards
beats.narration: Now extract two similar prompts into cards and ask Claude to merge them into a single canonical prompt card that covers both use cases.

props.command: claude "Merge these two prompt cards into one canonical card covering both use cases. Preserve all constraints. Generalize the task statement. Note both use cases in Version Notes." < card_a.md card_b.md
props.runningText: merging two cards into canonical card…

beats.label: Merged card output
beats.narration: The merged card generalizes the task statement, preserves all constraints from both versions, and notes both use cases in the Version Notes field. One card replaces two.

shots.notes: Three cards: Card A + Card B -> MERGED CANONICAL CARD. Arrow diagram. Merged card shows 'Task: [generalized]', 'Constraints: [union of both]', 'Version Notes: covers use case A and B.' Green checkmark on merged card. Teardown palette.

beats.label: Teardown angle
beats.narration: The prompt card is the difference between a prompt that works once and a prompt that's a shared asset. The 8 fields capture everything needed to reuse, adapt, and explain the prompt without re-deriving it.

shots.notes: Large text: 'From disposable to shared asset.' Below: 'Works once: prompt in chat history.' / 'Shared asset: prompt card with 8 fields.' CRIMSON on 'disposable.' Green on 'shared asset.' CRIMSON rule. Teardown palette.

beats.label: Series close
beats.narration: That's the full Prompt Engineering series — 9 CLI tools, 9 teardown angles. The work order is the prompt. Make it count.

shots.notes: Clean: '9 of 9 — Prompt Engineering Series Complete.' Below list of 9 slug titles in small text. CRIMSON accent line. Teardown palette.
